This document includes the account of Shelomoh (Solomon) becoming king, then the rebellion of the northern tribes against his son Rehoboam becoming king. That rebellion was led by Yeroboam (Jeroboam) and resulted in the split between the northern kingdom (called вҖҳYisrael/IsraelвҖҷ or sometimes вҖҳEfrayim/EphraimвҖҷ) and the southern king (called вҖҳYehudah/JudahвҖҷ and consisting mostly of the tribes of Yehudah and Benyamin). This document then ends with the accounts of the kingdom of Yehudah (Judah) and the defeat of Yerushalem (Jerusalem) in 586 B.C.
